text: Ɔkaa yei sɛ efiri sɛ na bɛɛkeka sɛ, “Ɔwɔ honhom fi.” Yesu Abusua kann

ref: Joseph was a good man and did not want to embarrass Mary in front of everyone. So he decided to quietly call off the wedding.

translated: Now Joseph was a righteous man and not wanting to expose Mary to public disgrace, he decided to send her away quietly.

ref: But when Herod heard about Jesus, he said, “This must be John! I had his head cut off, and now he has come back to life.”

translated: Then Herod heard of it, he said, “Behold! John, whom I beheaded, has risen from the dead?”

text: “Akoa biaa ntumi nsom awuranom mmienu; efiri sɛ ɔbɛtane baako na wadɔ baako, anaa ɔde ne ho bɛbata baako na waabu baako animtia. Hontumi nsom Nyankopɔn ne ahonyadeɛ.” Honnwennwene (Luka
translated: "Akoa teaches our generation; for he will give one and take one, or he will take one and leave one behind. He teaches us about God and mercy." Parable (Luke)

ref: One Sabbath Jesus and his disciples were walking through some wheat fields. His disciples were picking grains of wheat as they went along.

translated: And when the woman entered a house with Jesus in a certain village, and they came to her, her disciples told her everything that was said regarding her sick child.

ref: “The Emperor's,” they answered. Then Jesus told them, “Give the Emperor what belongs to him and give God what belongs to God.”
text: Bebuaa sɛ, “Kaesare dea.” Na ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Saa deɛ a, homfa deɛ ɔyɛ Kaesare deɛ mma Kaesare, na homfa deɛ ɔyɛ Nyankopɔn deɛ mma Nyankopɔn.”
translated: Bebuaa said, "Caesar's coin." And he answered and said to them, "Render what is Caesar's to Caesar, and render what is God's to God."

ref: Pilate knew the leaders had brought Jesus to him because they were jealous.
text: Obisaa yei efiri sɛ, ohuu sɛ anibereɛ nti ne bɛde Yesu bɛhyɛɛ ne nsa.

ref: He went into the house of God, and then they ate the sacred loaves of bread that only priests are supposed to eat.
text: Owuraa Nyankopɔn fie, na odii Kyerɛ Paanoo he a mmara mma kwane mma ɔno anaa ne nkurofoɔ he nso nni, gye sɛ asɔfoɔ nkoaa.
translated: The house of Sir Nyankopɔn, and she gave birth to Kyerɛ Paanoo, who performed the custom for the Kwane for them, or there are also his children there, like a certain priest.

ref: Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that. He said, “God would never let this happen to you, Lord!”
text: Na Petro de no gyinaa nkyɛne na ofirii aseɛ kaa n’anim sɛ, “Awurade! Yei mpare wo. Yei ntumi nto wo da!”
translated: And Peter stood up in his place and began to speak, saying, "Lord! We have found you. We have not seen your face!"

ref: On the other hand, these people think John was a prophet, and we are afraid of what they might do to us. That's why we can't say it was merely some human who gave John the right to baptize.”
text: Nso sɛ yɛka sɛ, ‘Ofiri nnipa,’ a yesuro nea nnipadɔm he bɛyɔ; efiri sɛ bɛ nyinaa bebu Yohane sɛ diyifoɔ.”
translated: Then he said to them, "Send the people away," because all the people were following John the Baptist.

ref: When you pray, don't be like those show-offs who love to stand up and pray in the synagogues and on the street corners. They do this just to look good. I can assure you that they already have their reward.
text: “Na sɛ hoobɔ mpaeɛ a honnyɔ sɛ nyaatwomfoɔ he; efiri sɛ bɛpɛ sɛ begyina hyiadane mu ne mmɔntene ntweaso bɔ mpaeɛ, sɛ nnipa behu bɛ. Nɔkorɛ, meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, bɛanya bɛ akatua dada.

text: Na nea beguu no asaase pa mu he gyina hɔ ma nnipa a bɛte asɛm he a bɛgye na bɛsõ aba mmɔho aduasa ne aduosia ne mmɔho ha.” Kanea a ɔhyɛ Atam Ase
translated: And the thing that was first, he caused people to come forth from the good earth, and the command he gave and the words he spoke, he called the names Adoasa and Aduosia and the name Ha. This is what he made, Atam Ase.

ref: He taught them like someone with authority, and not like their teachers of the Law of Moses.
text: efiri sɛ ɔkyerɛkyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ bi a ɔwɔ tumi, na ɔnyɛ sɛnea bɛ atwerɛfoɔ he kyerɛkyerɛ he.
translated: Because he declared that someone has authority, and he is not the one who will obey him when he instructs him.

ref: It is like what happens when a mustard seed is planted in the ground. It is the smallest seed in all the world.
text: Ɔte sɛ sinapi aba a sɛ bedua ne asaase mu a, ɔno ne aba a osua paa asaase so aba nyinaa mu;
translated: When it is sown in the earth like a mustard seed, it becomes the greatest among all seeds on the earth;
